topic: AI Terminal Coding Tools 2026: Claude Code vs Codex CLI vs Gemini CLI vs OpenCode

competitors.title: OpenCode vs Claude Code vs Cursor: AI Coding Agents Compared (2026)
key_points: Three tools dominate AI coding agent conversation in 2026: OpenCode (open-source terminal powerhouse routing to 75+ LLM providers), Claude Code (Anthropic's opinionated CLI agent built around highest-scoring model on SWE-bench), and Cursor (VS Code fork AI IDE)

OpenCode offers model flexibility via Models.dev integration (75+ providers), Go TUI performance, Oh-My-OpenAgent extension with 10 specialized agents, Hashline edit tool for reliable file modifications, native LSP support for code intelligence

Claude Code bets on one model family (Claude) with SWE-bench 80.8% score, deep git integration (auto-commit, PR creation), hooks system for project-specific rules, subagent spawning for research tasks, background agents for long tasks

Cursor provides full IDE experience with visual diff reviews, tab completion, Composer multi-file editing, lower learning curve for VS Code users, but has Electron overhead and limited agentic capability
OpenCode pricing: free + API costs, cheapest option with local models via Ollama. Claude Code: $20/month (Pro) or $100/month (Max). Cursor: $20/month (Pro) or $40/month (Business)
Best for: OpenCode for power users, multi-model workflows, DevOps engineers; Claude Code for deep codebase work, agentic tasks; Cursor for visual developers, IDE-first workflows

competitors.title: Claude Code vs Codex CLI vs Gemini CLI: Which AI Terminal Agent Wins in 2026?
key_points: Claude Code: Most capable AI terminal agent in 2026 with deepest code reasoning, best multi-file editing, proven multi-agent Code Review system, extended thinking feature
Codex CLI: Best free open-source option with autonomous task execution in sandboxed environments, parallel task support, cloud sandboxes + local execution, Apache 2.0 license
Gemini CLI: Wins on context window size (1M tokens) and free-tier generosity (180K completions/month + 240 chats/day), ideal for large codebases and budget-conscious developers
Context windows: Gemini CLI: 1M tokens, Claude Code: 200K tokens, Codex CLI: 128K-200K tokens
Pricing: Gemini CLI free tier, Claude Code $20/month (Pro), Codex CLI $20/month (ChatGPT Plus) or open-source with API costs
Multi-file editing leader: Claude Code can make coordinated changes across dozens of files while maintaining consistency through sub-agent system

Git integration: Claude Code has deepest integration (branches, commits, PRs, merge conflicts), Codex CLI tight GitHub integration with @codex triggers, Gemini CLI provides GitHub integration but less deep
MCP support: Claude Code as protocol creator has most mature ecosystem, Codex CLI and Gemini CLI also support MCP but with smaller ecosystems
Sandboxing: Codex CLI has strongest sandboxing with cloud isolated sandbox environments, Claude Code uses permission-based system, Gemini CLI minimal safety features
